thread.title: Bill Clinton says being called first Black president was 'great compliment'
thread.title_full: Bill Clinton says being called first Black president was 'great compliment'

author: JACKSON WALKER | The National News Desk
title: Bill Clinton says being called first Black president was 'great compliment'
text: {p}Former President Bill Clinton on Thursday said he was flattered to be described as the country’s first Black president, adding that he viewed the title as a compliment.{/p}{p}The late Toni Morrison bestowed the title on Clinton in a 1998 piece for {em}The New Yorker{/em}. She claimed Clinton embodied several characteristics of Black people.{/p}{p}“After all, Clinton displays almost every trope of blackness: single-parent household, born poor, working-class, saxophone-playing, McDonald’s-and-junk-food-loving boy from Arkansas,” Morrison wrote. “And when virtually all the African-American Clinton appointees began, one by one, to disappear, when the President’s body, his privacy, his unpoliced sexuality became the focus of the persecution, when he was metaphorically seized and body-searched, who could gainsay these black men who knew whereof they spoke?”{/p}{p}Clinton revisited the remark during a book promotion event, saying he was honored by it.{/p}{p}“It was halfway serious and halfway making fun of me,” Clinton said of Morrison’s characterization of him. “I took that as a great compliment.”{/p}{p}“It wasn’t that I owe it to my ancestors because I grew up in a white, Southern, working-class family where my grandfather owned a country store and all of his customers were Black, and he loved them and they loved him,” he added.{/p}{p}Clinton continued by describing how his grandfather would often allow Black customers to take food without paying if they were unable to provide for themselves, as the country had not yet created the federal food stamp system.{/p}{p}Sen. Bernie Sanders, I-Vt., in 2016 jokingly described former President Barack Obama as America’s second Black president in reference to Morrison’s comment.{/p}{p}“First black president right?” Sen. Sanders asked {em}The Hollywood Reporter{/em}. “Obama is only the second black president.”{/p}{p}“This president will go down in history as one of the smartest presidents we had in this country,” Sen. Sanders added of Obama. “I think people are beginning to catch on. The more they hear from Republicans, the smarter they think he is.”{/p}{p}Morrison told {em}Time Magazine{/em} in 2008 readers misunderstood her decision to describe Clinton as she had.{/p}{p}“People misunderstood that phrase,” she said. “I was deploring the way in which President Clinton was being treated, vis-à-vis the sex scandal that was surrounding him. I said he was being treated like a black on the street, already guilty, already a perp. I have no idea what his real instincts are, in terms of race.”{/p}{p}{em}{a href=\"https://twitter.com/_jlwalker_\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://twitter.com/_jlwalker_\"}Follow {/a}Jackson Walker on X at @_jlwalker_ for the latest trending national news. Have a news tip? Send it to jacwalker@sbgtv.com.{/em}{/p}{p}We warm up a little more on Friday ahead of the weekend winds...{/p}{p}TODAY: Expect mostly sunny skies in Las Vegas with high temperatures reaching 65 degrees this afternoon. Winds will be light up to 10 mph. Highs will max out in the low 60s in Summerlin & up to 67 degrees in Sunrise Manor in the east valley.{/p}{p}Air quality is 'good' to 'moderate' for pockets of haze and pollen levels are 'low.'{/p}{p}TONIGHT: Skies will be mostly clear this evening with overnight valley low temperatures dropping to around 46 degrees. Lows will be in the low 40s on the edges of town. Breezes will start picking up after midnight with overnight wind gusts 20-30 mph.{/p}{p}EXTENDED FORECAST: Unsettled weather arrives this weekend with winds ramping up on Saturday. A WIND ADVISORY will be in effect from 1:00 am Saturday to 4:00 am Sunday for portions of southern Nye County & Clark County including the Spring Mountains & Red Rock Canyon. Sustained winds will be out of the southwest 25-35 mph with wind gusts up to 60 mph. Neighborhoods in the foothills of the Spring Mountains may also see wind gusts 40+ mph from strong downslope winds.{/p}{p}The Formula 1 forecast calls for temperatures in the low 60s around 10pm Saturday when the main race begins! Winds will be noticeable, picking up out of the south/southwest 10-20 mph with wind gusts up to around 30-35 mph late Saturday night.{/p}{p}Breezes will linger on Sunday with a slight chance of mountain showers. The snow level will drop to around 6,500 to 7,000 ft so we may see some mixed precipitation in the high terrain.{/p}{p}Our next weather makers will be a few disturbances passing through the West Coast next week. Valley rain shower chances will be possible Monday through Wednesday with the best odds of seeing wet weather on Wednesday. As of Friday, it has been 132 days since Las Vegas has received measurable rain at the airport, the official weather reporting site for the valley. As of right now most rain moving in next week will be very light as most moisture will get trapped up in the Sierra as storms pass through.{/p}{p}Shower chances will range from 20%-30% in Las Vegas & Henderson next Monday through Wednesday with better rain/snow chances in the mountains (about 40%-50% odds).{/p}{p}On Thanksgiving, drier weather is anticipated with high temperatures around 59 degrees in Las Vegas.{/p}{p}Beyond the 7-day forecast, temperatures will range from 58-61 degrees NEXT Friday through Sunday under mostly sunny to partly cloudy skies.{/p}{p}Stay with News 3 for more updates on-air and online & have a great weekend!{/p}{p}- Chloe Koast{/p}{p}{br}{/p}{p}{br}{/p}{p}{br}{/p}{p}Former President Bill Clinton on Thursday said he was flattered to be described as the country’s first Black president, adding that he viewed the title as a compliment.{/p}{p}The late Toni Morrison bestowed the title on Clinton in a 1998 piece for {em}The New Yorker{/em}. She claimed Clinton embodied several characteristics of Black people.{/p}{p}“After all, Clinton displays almost every trope of blackness: single-parent household, born poor, working-class, saxophone-playing, McDonald’s-and-junk-food-loving boy from Arkansas,” Morrison wrote. “And when virtually all the African-American Clinton appointees began, one by one, to disappear, when the President’s body, his privacy, his unpoliced sexuality became the focus of the persecution, when he was metaphorically seized and body-searched, who could gainsay these black men who knew whereof they spoke?”{/p}{p}Clinton revisited the remark during a book promotion event, saying he was honored by it.{/p}{p}“It was halfway serious and halfway making fun of me,” Clinton said of Morrison’s characterization of him. “I took that as a great compliment.”{/p}{p}“It wasn’t that I owe it to my ancestors because I grew up in a white, Southern, working-class family where my grandfather owned a country store and all of his customers were Black, and he loved them and they loved him,” he added.{/p}{p}Clinton continued by describing how his grandfather would often allow Black customers to take food without paying if they were unable to provide for themselves, as the country had not yet created the federal food stamp system.{/p}{p}Sen. The more they hear from Republicans, the smarter they think he is.”{/p}{p}Morrison told {em}Time Magazine{/em} in 2008 readers misunderstood her decision to describe Clinton as she had.{/p}{p}“People misunderstood that phrase,” she said. “I was deploring the way in which President Clinton was being treated, vis-à-vis the sex scandal that was surrounding him. I said he was being treated like a black on the street, already guilty, already a perp. I have no idea what his real instincts are, in terms of race.”{/p}{p}{em}{a href=\"https://twitter.com/_jlwalker_\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://twitter.com/_jlwalker_\"}Follow {/a}Jackson Walker on X at @_jlwalker_ for the latest trending national news. As of right now most rain moving in next week will be very light as most moisture will get trapped up in the Sierra as storms pass through.{/p}{p}Shower chances will range from 20%-30% in Las Vegas & Henderson next Monday through Wednesday with better rain/snow chances in the mountains (about 40%-50% odds).{/p}{p}On Thanksgiving, drier weather is anticipated with high temperatures around 59 degrees in Las Vegas.{/p}{p}Beyond the 7-day forecast, temperatures will range from 58-61 degrees NEXT Friday through Sunday under mostly sunny to partly cloudy skies.{/p}{p}Stay with News 3 for more updates on-air and online & have a great weekend!{/p}{p}- Chloe Koast{/p}{p}{br}{/p}{p}{br}{/p}{p}{br}{/p}
